observations.recommendation: We recommended, and the Municipal Engineer agreed to prepare pertinent documents relating to the demolished buildings and structures and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant as basis for its derecognition in the books of accounts of the LGU.

We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to conduct analysis on the negative carrying amount of the School Building account under the General Fund and make the necessary adjusting entry to correct the over-recording of depreciation expenses and the corresponding accumulated depreciation.

We recommended that Management:
Allocate funds for the titling of lands;
Facilitate the annotation of the Deeds in the subject Titles in the Register of Deeds; and
Facilitate the processing of Land Titles.

We recommended, and the Municipal Assessor agreed to submit to the Municipal Accountant the pertinent documents relating to the donation of the subject parcels of land. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary journal entries to derecognize the recorded costs of these parcels of land in the books of accounts of the LGU.

We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the necessary journal entries to recognize in the books of accounts the unrecorded parcel of land duly registered and titled in the name of the LGU at its fair/market value and provide disclosures for the same in the notes to financial statements.

We recommended, and Management agreed to complete the application of pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006, dated January 31, 2020, and fully comply with the prescribed guidelines and procedures, particularly the conduct of physical count of PPE, the recognition of PPE items found at station, the disposition of non-existing/missing PPE items, the procedures in the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without available record of accountability, and the recording of accounting entries and disclosures in the notes to financial statements, as applicable, for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of the LGU.

We recommended, and:
The GSO agreed to prepare the SSMI together with the RIS relative to the issued supplies in CY 2024 and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant;
Henceforth, the GSO agreed to prepare the RIS for every issuance of supplies, consolidate the same into the SSMI, and regularly submit these reports to the Municipal Accountant as basis for the recording of expenses; and
The Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record the issuances of supplies made in CY 2024 based on the SSMI or RIS submitted by the GSO.

We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to observe the Perpetual Inventory Method by initially recording the purchases of supplies and materials as inventories and subsequently recognize the same as expenses upon issuance/distribution to end-users/intended beneficiaries.

We recommended, and:
The MSWDO agreed to submit to the Municipal Accountant the pertinent reports and documents related to the goods already distributed and goods which still remained in stock;
Henceforth, the MSWDO agreed to regularly prepare and submit said reports to the Municipal Accountant as basis for the recording of expenses; and
The Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record the total amount of the remaining goods in stock as Welfare Goods for Distribution.

We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to carefully review and analyze transactions involving semi-expendable property and record the same into its appropriate inventory/expense accounts.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant conduct analysis of the Accounts Payable and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the affected balances into its appropriate liability accounts;
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant ensure that succeeding payables are classified and recorded into their appropriate liability accounts; and
Management determine the validity of the subject accounts payable aged two years and above, and send final notices to concerned offices/claimants to facilitate the immediate payment of their claims, as warranted. Otherwise, consider the reversion of these accounts to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund.

We recommended, and Management agreed to make representations with identified source agencies for the return of unutilized balances and/or reversion of the same to the National Treasury, as warranted. Henceforth, facilitate the return of unutilized balances to concerned agencies and/or revert the same to the National Treasury, as appropriate. Otherwise, Management initiate the request for write-off of dormant Due to NGAs account balances in accordance with the guidelines and procedures set forth under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended, and:
Management agreed to make representations with J.H. Cerilles State College Main Campus pertaining to the remittance or transfer of the outstanding balance in the books of accounts of the LGU; and
Pending the result of representation with J.H. Cerilles State College Main Campus, the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the necessary adjusting entry to reclassify the outstanding balances from the Due to NGAs account to Other Payables account.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant make representations with the DBP Pagadian Branch relative to the balance of the Cash in Bank – LCCA with account no. 0840-019233-080, conduct reconciliation of balances with the bank, and record the necessary adjustments in the LGU’s books of accounts, as warranted; and
The Municipal Treasurer prepare and maintain a cashbook for the subject depository account.

We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the accounting error made by debiting the Other Deferred Credits account and crediting the Advances to Contractors account in the amount of P152,067.79; and
Henceforth, ensure that the recoupments of advance payments are properly recorded and classified in the books of accounts of the LGU.

We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Prepare the corresponding reciprocal entries to the appropriate funds relative to the journal entries made affecting the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts in the books amounting to P80,888.50;
Conduct regular reconciliation of balances between the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts to eliminate the occurrence of variances or discrepancies between the reciprocal accounts; and
Initiate the return or settlement of government funds from one Fund to another, as appropriate, to come up with zero balances of the said accounts.

We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to present the effects of changes in accounting policy and prior period errors/adjustments by restating the prior year’s balances of the affected accounts shown as corresponding figures in the current year comparative FS in compliance with the requirements of IPSAS 3.

We recommended, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
Send notices/letters to concerned suppliers/contractors informing them of their outstanding/unreleased retention money which can be claimed subject to the submission of letter request for the release of the same. Henceforth, initiate the processing of claims for the release of the retention money due to suppliers/contractors. Otherwise, Management may consider the forfeiture and reversion of these accounts to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund if found to be without valid claims or if the same remains outstanding for over a long period of time due to inaction by concerned suppliers/contractors; and
Verify records and examine the cash deficit or variances totaling P120,378.07 between the reported Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable and the corresponding Cash in Bank – LCCA relative to the subject two infrastructure projects in the Trust Fund. Henceforth, effect the necessary adjustments, if warranted.

We recommended, and Management, through its LDRRM Council and LDRRM Officer, agreed to:
Ensure optimal utilization of the LDRRM Fund and observe proper/timely implementation of disaster risk reduction and management PPAs;
Fast track the completion and/or implementation of disaster risk reduction and management PPAs funded out of the continuing appropriations and special trust fund which remained ongoing and/or unimplemented as of year-end. Otherwise, consider the reprogramming of the same which may no longer be needed into other priority disaster risk reduction and management PPAs; and
For fully completed/implemented PPAs, realign or reprogram the excess balances of continuing appropriations of the LDRRM Fund to finance other priority disaster risk reduction and management PPAs of the LGU.

We recommended, and:
The Local Chief Executive agreed to organize and establish the CCT who shall be responsible for the formulation, refinement, finalization, testing, implementation, evaluation, packaging, updating, and improvement of their organization’s PSCP; and
The Management, through its CCT, agreed to finalize its PSCP in accordance with the PSCP Guidebook 2nd Edition, and submit the same to the DND-OCD through the CBTS for consolidation and databasing.

We recommended, and Management agreed to:
Develop and implement clear internal guidelines for determining eligibility for the grant of hazard pay including the documentary requirements for the processing of such claims, ensuring its alignment with the provisions of RA No. 9433 and DBM-DSWD Joint Circular No. 2018-01; and
Ensure that payments of hazard pay be made only during the duration of the actual assignment of the social workers in work areas or situations which exposed them to hazards and/or hardships. This includes identifying the specific dates when the social workers are assigned to work areas or situations which exposed them to hazards and/or hardships together with corresponding valid accomplishment reports.

We recommended, and Management agreed to:
In coordination with the Municipal Engineer and concerned contractors, submit to the Office of the Auditor the as-built plans of the subject infrastructure projects for audit and evaluation;
Require contractors of their submission of warranty securities for completed and accepted infrastructure projects, in order to protect not only the interest of the government but also those project beneficiaries and other third parties involved in the execution of government projects; and
Through the Municipal Accountant, ensure that final payments for infrastructure projects are supported with complete documentation as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended, and Management agreed to require contractors the submission of an irrevocable standby letter of credit of equivalent value from a commercial bank, a bank guarantee or a surety bond callable upon demand to support its claim/request for advance payment for mobilization. Henceforth, ensure that advance payments for mobilization relative to infrastructure projects are supported with complete documentation as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended, and Management agreed to:
Grant contract time extensions after careful examination of the complete and sufficient documents such as, but not limited to, the MTSR or any similar document for contract time extensions due to unfavorable weather condition, and the certification from appropriate government agencies for contract time extensions due to shortage or non-availability of materials as provided under DPWH DO No. 42 s.2020, and Annex “E” of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, respectively;
Ensure proper conduct of detailed engineering activities particularly the conduct of field survey and investigation, preparation of PERT/CPM network diagram and the computation of contract time or duration; and
Require contractors to cause the extension of their performance securities corresponding the contract time extensions granted to them.

We recommended, and the LSB agreed to:
Submit quarterly and annual reports using the SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 (Annex B of DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017) on SEF utilization, as prepared by the Local Accountant, to the DepEd Central Office, through appropriate channels, copy furnished the local sanggunian concerned and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG, within the indicative timeline; and
Ensure the posting of the SEF Utilization Reports in at least three conspicuous public places not later than the 20th day after the end of each quarter as required under DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017 and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2024-021.

We recommended, and Management agreed to correct the noted deficiencies and comply with the fire safety measures prescribed under Rule 10, Chapter 2 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9514 to ensure the safety of the occupants of government buildings and structures and to prevent the risks of damage or loss of life and property.

We recommended and management agreed to the following:

The MDC reprogram the balance of debt servicing amounting to P7.147 million to other viable development projects, subject to the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan;

The Municipal Engineer and MPDC to fast track the implementation of the PAPs that were already under contract; and

The BAC, Municipal Engineer, MPDC and MDC properly plan and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects to pave the way for their timely implementation and completion.

We recommended and management agreed to the following:

The LCE, thru the HRMO, formulate a uniform policy or guidelines in the hiring of JO/COS personnel taking into consideration the LGU’s existing organizational structure, plantilla of personnel, service requirements, financial capability and existing COA and CSC rules and regulations; and endorse the same to the Office of the Sangguniang Bayan for legislation;

The Sangguniang Bayan, upon review and deliberation, institutionalize the policy or guidelines in the hiring of JO/COS personnel by legislating it into an ordinance; and 

Revisit and assess its existing organizational structure in order to establish the number of personnel needed or necessary in each office/department, and evaluate whether the duties and responsibilities of the hired JO/COS personnel correlate with the principal mandate of the assigned office/department, to avoid the hiring of excessive and/or unnecessary number of JO/COS personnel.

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:

The Municipal Development Council (MDC) realign the residual balances of completed projects to other viable developmental projects, subject to the approval of the Sangguniang Bayan;

We recommended and Management agreed to the following:

The LDRRMO include, in a separate caption, the PPAs to be charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years or those which were transferred to the Trust Fund; and

The Municipal Accountant create and maintain a special account in the general fund for the receipt and utilization of the BGCM with complete and separate subsidiary ledgers, trial balance and financial statements.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee observe the prevailing wholesale price monitored by independent agencies when procuring in bulk, especially in procurement through emergency purchases.


Further for the end-user or the duly authorized official or personnel to ensure that due diligence is exercised and evaluation of costs is performed in determining the appropriate price/costs for the proposed procurement of goods and the ABC of the goods is a valid price to prevent excessive price/costing, moving forward.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor take charge of the implementation of basic services including granting of assistance through the concerned offices of the municipality. The SB shall concentrate on legislative functions and strictly confine their acts within the limits of their mandated powers, duties and function in accordance with the provision cited in Section 477 of RA No. 7160.

The BAC and TWG efficiently conduct post-qualification of bidders to verify, validate, and ascertain all statements made by the bidder. In case of any misrepresentation made in the eligibility requirements, statements, or documents, which will affect the capability of the bidder to undertake the project, consider the said bidder as ineligible and disqualify the latter from obtaining an award or contract.

observations.managementAction: Management  stopped hiring contract of services and job order to perform functions pertaining to vacant plantilla positions and  strictly complied with the rules and regulations on the contract of services, particularly stipulations made in the Job Orders/ Appointments to comply with the Civil Service Commission Resolution No. 020790 dated June 5, 2002
observations.reason: Per verification made,  LGU-Lapuyan did not hire contract of service and job order to perform funactions pertaining to vacant plantilla positions.  Also, strictly complied with the rules and regulations on the contract of services, particularly stipulations made in the Job Orders/ Appointments.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Human Resource Management Officer and the Municipal Accountant ascertain that all the required supporting documents are complied, computations are correct and all data are properly filled up pursuant to Section 23, Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of the Administrative Code of 1987, also known as the Omnibus Rules on Leave.
observations.managementAction: Management corrected the erroneous deduction from the sick leave of the number of days monetized thru restoration  number of days to the sick leave and a deduction to vacation leave.  All documents required will be submitted.
observations.reason: In CY 2016 the required supporting documents for the monetization of accumulated leave credits were submitted.  Further, necessary  corrections as to restoration of numbers of days to the sick leave and deduction to vacation leave were made.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer pay electric bill on time to avoid incurrence of penalty/surcharges. For the surcharges which have been due already, the persons responsible for its incurrence shall be made to refund for the same.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor instructed the Municipal Treasurer to create control measures in payment of monthly bill to avoid delays of payment.
observations.reason: In CY 2016, management did not incur surcharges relative to the payment of monthly electric bill.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the annual appropriations for the JHCSC-Lapuyan Extension Campus and LWSS be included in the appropriation ordinance of LGU-Lapuyan and be enacted by its Sangguniang Bayan.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendation that the annual appropriations for the JHCSC-Lapuyan Extension Campus and LWSS be included in the appropriation ordinance of LGU-Lapuyan and be enacted by its Sangguniang Bayan
observations.reason: The annual appropriations for the JHCSC-Lapuyan Extension Campus and LWSS were included in the appropriation ordinance of LGU-Lapuyan and will be enacted by its Sangguniang Bayan.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Municipal Treasurer enforce the remedies and measures prescribed under Section 254 and 258 of RA 7160 to collect delinquent real property taxes. Strengthen collection system through intensified tax campaign to eliminate and/or minimize delinquencies.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal government committed to create necessary measures to collect delinquent real property taxes.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer  facilitate the immediate remittance of withholding taxes and contributions particularly those pertaining to prior years due to BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and Philhealth within the prescribed period to enable these agencies to use the needed funds for its operations in a timely manner and to preclude delay in the processing of members/ employees’ claims and prevent possible forfeiture of the benefits due them to protect their interest.
observations.managementAction: Management see to it that funds withheld for the BIR, GSIS, PAG-ibig and PhilHealth were remitted on time.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: Various reports for LDRRMF should be prepared with the correct amount of 30% Quick Response Fund and 70% Mitigation Fund and submitted within the required period for the proper allocation and accounting of the disaster risk reduction management projects, programs and activities to be implemented and monitored.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM Officer will make the necessary reports on the Fund Utilization of LDRRMF for CY 2016 and submit the same to COA.
observations.reason: Management only prepared quarterly report on the utilization of LDRRMF and posted the same in the DILG Full Disclosure Portal and in three conspicuous places in the municipality but failed to submit the same to COA.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant shall prepare and submit the financial statements within the prescribed period to facilitate verification, analysis and audit of the accounts.  Also, the Municipal Budget Officer shall prepare and submit the monthly Status of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations.
observations.managementAction: Management exerted efforts and adopted control measures for the submission of the required documents on time.
observations.reason: Monthly Trial Balance and Status of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (SAAO) were prepared and submitted monthly except for SAOB.

observations.recommendation: Require the Drivers and other concerned personnel to prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Official Travel and the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption pursuant to Section 361 (g) of COA Circular No. 91-368 and COA Circular No. 77-61 dated September 26, 1977 for effective control and evaluation. The Monthly Report of Official Travel will be the summary of trips per vehicle as reflected in chronological order of the serially numbered Driver’s Trip Tickets.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor, through a memorandum will designate personnel to do the consolidation of all trip tickets and consumption reports and prepare and submit the Monthly Report on Fuel Consumption.
observations.reason: Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travel relative to the consumed fuel totaling P2,789,009.29 were not submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that an inventory committee be created pursuant to Sec 490, GAAM Vol. I, physical inventory taking be made, the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) be prepared and submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

The Municipal Treasurer and Accountant should likewise update the   Property Cards and PPE Ledger Cards, respectively, per  category of property, plant and equipment, as required under Sections 119 and 120 of Volume I of the NGAS Manual.
observations.managementAction: Management created an inventory committee, who will ensure that a physical inventory count of all property, plant and equipment will be conducted as soon as possible.
observations.reason: Management was not able to provide the report. Inventory committee was created but was not able to conduct the inventory count of PPE.
